text.title: Cloud Run Monitoring Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the cloud-run-monitoring methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Cloud Run applications MUST emit structured JSON log entries to stdout with at minimum `severity`, `message`, and a request `trace` field.
rule.rationale: Cloud Logging auto-parses JSON from stdout into structured fields; unstructured logs cannot be filtered by field nor power log-based metrics.

rule.statement: Logs MUST NOT contain credentials, tokens, or PII.
rule.rationale: Cloud Logging is broadly readable by viewers; logged secrets leak across roles and end up indexed in BigQuery sinks.

rule.statement: Every production service MUST have alerting policies on 5xx rate, p99 request latency, instance count, and CPU utilisation.
rule.rationale: These four signals catch the dominant failure modes (deploy failure, cold-start tail, traffic burst, resource saturation) without requiring custom instrumentation.

rule.statement: Service-to-service calls MUST propagate `X-Cloud-Trace-Context` so Cloud Trace can reconstruct cross-service spans.
rule.rationale: Without header propagation traces fracture into disconnected spans and cross-service root-cause analysis becomes impossible.

rule.statement: Notification channels (PagerDuty, email, Pub/Sub) MUST be wired up and tested before the service serves production traffic.
rule.rationale: Alerts firing into a black hole offer no detection value; pre-launch verification of channels is the only way to know paging actually reaches a human.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
